label: Retry with Backoff
description: Wraps a downstream operation and retries it with exponential backoff on failure. Returns the first successful result; throws a clear error naming the last failure once all retries are exhausted.

settings.name: maxRetries
settings.label: Max retries
settings.description: Number of additional attempts after the first. maxRetries=3 means up to 4 total attempts. 0 disables retrying.

settings.name: baseDelayMs
settings.label: Base delay (ms)
settings.description: Delay before the first retry. Subsequent retries multiply this by backoffFactor each time (capped at maxDelayMs).

settings.name: maxDelayMs
settings.label: Max delay (ms)
settings.description: Upper bound on any single backoff sleep. The exponential delay is clamped to this ceiling.

settings.name: backoffFactor
settings.label: Backoff factor
settings.description: Multiplier applied to the delay each retry. 2 = double each time (500ms, 1s, 2s, …). 1 = constant delay.

settings.name: jitter
settings.label: Jitter
settings.description: When true, each backoff delay is randomized within [delay/2, delay] (full jitter) to de-correlate concurrent retriers. Disable for deterministic delays.

ai.shortDescription: Retry a flaky downstream operation with exponential backoff before giving up.
ai.whenToUse: Place upstream of an http_request / mcp_tool / agent node that calls a flaky third-party API. The downstream subgraph runs once per attempt; on the first success the flow continues, otherwise the node errors after maxRetries.
ai.examplePrompt: retry the API call up to 5 times with exponential backoff starting at 1 second
ai.promptHints: Output envelope: {{steps.X.result.ok}} (true on success), {{steps.X.result.attempts}} (how many tries it took). On exhaustion the node throws — route its error port to an error_handler to recover.

outputAssertions.name: retry_envelope
outputAssertions.errorMessage: retry_with_backoff output is missing the success envelope { ok, attempts }.
